steps.text: Review available documentation (Annual Report, experiment logs, safety review committee minutes, or experiment proposals) prior to the onsite inspection.
Review the licensee's procedures for the control and conduct of experiments prior to the onsite inspection.

Conduct direct observation of 50 percent of the new experiments or changes to experiments since the last inspection (or review records and discuss with cognizant personnel if direct observation is not practical).

Review the information available to the operator that authorizes the conduct of a particular experiment (such as a proposal with signatures, an experiment review form, or operating log entries) to determine compliance with TS and procedural requirements.

preconditions.text: Documentation is already available at the office.
Reviewing procedures prior to the onsite inspection is practical.

constraints.text: The inspector is not to review experiments or changes to experiments that have been previously reviewed by NRC personnel.

steps.text: Obtain approval for routine experiments (such as gold foil irradiation) from the reactor supervisor, a licensed senior reactor operator (SRO), or the radiation control officer.
Document routine experiments individually for each new group of experimenters, or whenever the experiment has not been performed for one calendar year or more by the original experimenter.
Obtain approval for documented routine experiments from the reactor supervisor, an SRO, or the radiation control officer.
Obtain approval from the safety review committee, reactor supervisor, SRO, and radiation control officer for experiments that could pose a hazard.
Verify that all expressed conditions are met before conducting the experiment.
Obtain approval from the safety review committee, reactor supervisor, SRO, and radiation control officer for experiments with significant hazard potential.
Insert, disassemble, or remove the experiment from the reactor under the direct supervision of the reactor supervisor or a duly authorized SRO.

preconditions.text: The routine experiment must pose no hazard to the reactor, the personnel, or the public.
A written description of reactor operations (from insertion until removal) approved by the safety review committee is required before an experiment is inserted in the reactor.

constraints.text: Particular attention is to be given to experiments that contain special nuclear material (fueled experiments) or explosives.
The irradiation of explosives requires NRC review and approval by the Office of Nuclear Reactor Regulation (NRR) and must be written into the facility TS.

steps.text: Monitor gauges that measure pressures, including vacuum conditions, where appropriate to the experimental setup.
Verify that the applicable TS limits for reactivity, such as shutdown margin, excess reactivity, and individual and total worth of experiments were not exceeded.
Stop the experiment and calculate the value to assure compliance with constraint limits and shutdown margin limits.
Verify the location and status of three past experiments performed under the license since the last inspection.
Control irradiated articles in accordance with 10 CFR Part 20 until final disposal.
Perform final disposal of irradiated articles in accordance with Subpart K of 10 CFR Part 20.

preconditions.text: If a regulating rod is changed by the established conservative cut-off amount

constraints.text: If the capsule has ruptured, the opening of the experiment tube could be radiologically hazardous or cause a reactivity transient.

steps.text: Discuss the methods of handling experiments.
Examine the process for consistency with the licensee’s administrative controls and procedures.

Determine how the licensee assures that experiments do not cause reactivity anomalies that cannot be controlled by the operator or control system, considering operator control over rabbit insertions/removals, experiments shadowing nuclear detectors, and experiments interfering with control rod/blade movement.
Verify if the licensee has considered the use of engineered safety features (such as remote handling devices, time, distance, and shielding) in the planning and conduct of experiments.

preconditions.text: Removal of an experiment from the reactor cannot be directly observed.

constraints.text: Experiments are assumed to be contaminated and highly radioactive until proven otherwise.

Apparatus and mechanisms other than controls, the operation of which may affect the reactivity or power level of a reactor, must be manipulated only with the knowledge and consent of a licensed operator or SRO.
Access to areas where radiation is present must be posted in accordance with 10 CFR Parts 20.1902 and 20.1903 and limited as required by the licensee’s radiation protection program.
